records.chapter: Reward of shaking hands
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from Muhammad b. Ali b. Muhammad b. al-Fudhayl from Abi Hamzah from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “The reward of shaking hands with each other is equal to the reward of a soldier in the way of Allah.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْفُضَیْلِ عَنْ أَبِی حَمْزَهَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: أَنْتُمْ فِی تَصَافُحِکُمْ فِی مِثْلِ أُجُورِ الْمُجَاهِدِینَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from Muhammad b. Ali b. Muhammad b. al-Fudhayl from Abi Hamzah from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “The reward of shaking hands with each other is equal to the reward of a soldier in the way of Allah.”

records.chapter: Reward of saying Bismillaah before meals
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Muhammad b. al-Hussain from Muhammad b. Yahya al-Khazaz from Ghiyath b. Ibrahim al-Darrami from Ja’far from his father from his forefathers from Ameer al-Mumineen (a.s.) who said: “One who utters the name of Allah before meals will never be questioned about that bounty of Allah.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ یَحْیَی الْخَزَّازِ عَنْ غِیَاثِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِیمَ الدَّارِمِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَنْ أَمِیرِ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع قَالَ: مَنْ ذَکَرَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَی طَعَامٍ لَمْ یُسْأَلْ عَنْ ذَلِکَ الطَّعَامِ أَبَداً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Muhammad b. al-Hussain from Muhammad b. Yahya al-Khazaz from Ghiyath b. Ibrahim al-Darrami from Ja’far from his father from his forefathers from Ameer al-Mumineen (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who utters the name of Allah before meals will never be questioned about that bounty of Allah.”

records.chapter: Reward of feeding a hungry person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. And from the previous chain from Ibrahim b. Ishaq from Muhammad b. Khalid from Uthman b. Isa from Sama’a from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Paradise is made obligatory for one who feeds a hungry person.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنْ سَمَاعَهَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ أَشْبَعَ کَبِداً جَائِعاً وَجَبَتْ لَهُ الْجَنَّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. And from the previous chain from Ibrahim b. Ishaq from Muhammad b. Khalid from Uthman b. Isa from Sama’a from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Paradise is made obligatory for one who feeds a hungry person.”

records.chapter: Reward of enjoying water
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. Abi (rah) from Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ya’qub b. Yazid from Ibn Fadhal who raised it to Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who enjoys tasting water in the world, Allah will make him enjoy pure wine in Paradise.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ یَعْقُوبَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنِ ابْنِ فَضَّالٍ یَرْفَعُهُ إِلَی أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ تَلَذَّذَ بِالْمَاءِ فِی الدُّنْیَا لَذَّذَهُ اللَّهُ مِنْ أَشْرِبَهِ الْجَنَّهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. Abi (rah) from Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ya’qub b. Yazid from Ibn Fadhal who raised it to Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who enjoys tasting water in the world, Allah will make him enjoy pure wine in Paradise.”

records.chapter: Reward of giving Sadaqah on Friday
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said, “My father was having less money  and  had  more  expenses  then  too  he  used  to  give Sadaqah  every  Friday.  He  used  to  say  that  the  reward  of Sadaqah on Friday is two-fold, for Friday is better than all other days.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبُو مُحَمَّدٍ الواشی [الْوَابِشِیُ] وَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ بُکَیْرٍ وَ غَیْرُهُ قَدْ رَوَاهُ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: کَانَ أَبِی ع أَقَلَّ أَهْلِ بَیْتِهِ مَالًا وَ أَعْظَمَهُمْ مَؤُنَهً قَالَ وَ کَانَ یَتَصَدَّقُ کُلَّ جُمُعَهٍ بِدِینَارٍ وَ کَانَ یَقُولُ الصَّدَقَهُ یَوْمَ الْجُمُعَهِ تُضَاعَفُ لِفَضْلِ الْجُمُعَهِ عَلَی غَیْرِهِ مِنَ الْأَیَّامِ.
records.thaqalaynMatn: “My father was having less money  and  had  more  expenses  then  too  he  used  to  give Sadaqah  every  Friday.  He  used  to  say  that  the  reward  of Sadaqah on Friday is two-fold, for Friday is better than all other days.”

records.chapter: Reward of helping sad people
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It is narrated that Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said, “One who helps  his  brother  who  is  thirsty  and  sorrowful,  solves  his problem and helps in fulfilling his desires the former will be granted  seventy-two  bounties,  which  will  keep  him  safe from fear on the Day of Judgment.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ زَیْدٍ الشَّحَّامِ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع یَقُولُ مَنْ أَعَانَ أَخَاهُ الْمُؤْمِنَ اللَّهْفَانَ عِنْدَ جَهْدِهِ فَنَفَّسَ کُرْبَتَهُ وَ أَعَانَهُ عَلَی نَجَاحِ حَاجَتِهِ کَانَتْ لَهُ بِذَلِکَ اثْنَتَانِ وَ سَبْعُونَ رَحْمَهً لِأَفْزَاعِ یَوْمِ الْقِیَامَهِ وَ أَهْوَالِهِ.

records.chapter: Reward of loving one’s brothers
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ahmad b. Khalid from Muhammad b. Ali from Umar b. Abd al-Aziz from Jameel b. Darraj from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “The excellence of a man near Allah is in loving his brother. Allah has put the love of brothers in his heart. Allah loves him and will grant him full reward on the Day of Judgment.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِیزِ عَنْ جَمِیلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مِنْ فَضْلِ الرَّجُلِ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ مَحَبَّتُهُ لِإِخْوَانِهِ وَ مَنْ عَرَّفَهُ اللَّهُ مَحَبَّهَ إِخْوَانِهِ أَحَبَّهُ اللَّهُ وَ مَنْ أَحَبَّهُ اللَّهُ وَفَّاهُ أَجْرَهُ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ahmad b. Khalid from Muhammad b. Ali from Umar b. Abd al-Aziz from Jameel b. Darraj from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “The excellence of a man near Allah is in loving his brother. Allah has put the love of brothers in his heart. Allah loves him and will grant him full reward on the Day of Judgment.”

records.chapter: Reward of fulfilling a desire of a person with whom Allah is pleased
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  narrates  from  his  predecessors, who narrate from Amirul Momineen (a.s.), who narrates that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “If someone fulfils a desire of a person with whom Allah is pleased the former will not go from this world till his own desire is fulfilled.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ التَّاجِرِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَهْزِیَارَ عَنْ فَضَالَهَ بْنِ أَیُّوبَ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِیلَ بْنِ أَبِی زِیَادٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَنْ أَمِیرِ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ تَمَنَّی شَیْئاً وَ هُوَ لِلَّهِ رِضًی لَمْ یَخْرُجْ مِنَ الدُّنْیَا حَتَّی یُعْطَاهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  narrates  from  his  predecessors, who narrate from Amirul Momineen (a.s.), who narrates that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “If someone fulfils a desire of a person with whom Allah is pleased the former will not go from this world till his own desire is fulfilled.”

records.chapter: Reward of meeting a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It is narrated that Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said, “One who goes to meet a Muslim brother, the Almighty Allah will call out  to  him,  ‘O  one  who  is  going  for  visiting!  You  have become pure and your reward is pure Paradise.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ سَعْدٍ عَنْ بَکْرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الْأَزْدِیِّ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع یَقُولُ مَا زَارَ مُسْلِمٌ أَخَاهُ فِی اللَّهِ إِلَّا نَادَاهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَیُّهَا الزَّائِرُ طِبْتَ وَ طَابَتْ لَکَ الْجَنَّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  It is narrated that Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who goes to meet a Muslim brother, the Almighty Allah will call out  to  him,  ‘O  one  who  is  going  for  visiting!  You  have become pure and your reward is pure Paradise.”

records.chapter: Reward of slaughtering an animal and distributing its meat among the poor after having built a house
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rh) narrated to me, he said: Ali bin Ibrahim, from his father, from an-Nawfali, from as-Sakuni, from Imam Ja’far bin Muhammad (a.s.), who narrates from his predecessors that the Messenger of Allah (sw) said:  “Whoever builds a house, slaughters a fat ram, distributes its meat among the poor and recites the following supplication: Allaahummadhar 'anni maradatal jinni wal insi wash shayaateeni wa baarik lee fee binaa'ee (O Allah, repel from me the harm of jinn, humans, and devils, and bless me in my building) will get whatever he wishes.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنِ النَّوْفَلِیِّ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ بَنَی مَسْکَناً فَذَبَحَ کَبْشاً سَمِیناً وَ أَطْعَمَ لَحْمَهُ الْمَسَاکِینَ ثُمَّ قَالَ اللَّهُمَّ ادْحَرْ عَنِّی مَرَدَهَ الْجِنِّ وَ الْإِنْسِ وَ الشَّیَاطِینِ وَ بَارِکْ لِی فِی بِنَائِی أُعْطِیَ مَا سَأَلَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rh) narrated to me, he said: Ali bin Ibrahim, from his father, from an-Nawfali, from as-Sakuni, from Imam Ja’far bin Muhammad (a.s.), who narrates from his predecessors that the Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Whoever builds a house, slaughters a fat ram, distributes its meat among the poor and recites the following supplication: Allaahummadhar 'anni maradatal jinni wal insi wash shayaateeni wa baarik lee fee binaa'ee (O Allah, repel from me the harm of jinn, humans, and devils, and bless me in my building) will get whatever he wishes.”

records.chapter: Reward of helping in doing good
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said I was informed by Abdillah b. Ja’far from Harun b. Muslim from Mas’ada b. Ziyad from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father from Ameer al-Mumineen (a.s.) that he said that The Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) who said: “Allah showers mercy on the father who helps his son in doing good. He showers mercy on a person who helps his neighbor in doing good. One who helps his friend in doing good Allah showers mercy on the former. Similarly one who helps his companion in doing good, Allah showers mercy on the former too. Also one who helps his master in doing good, Allah showers mercy on that person.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ مَسْعَدَهَ بْنِ زِیَادٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ أَمِیرِ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع أَنَّهُ قَالَ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ص قَالَ: رَحِمَ اللَّهُ وَالِداً أَعَانَ وَلَدَهُ عَلَی بِرِّهِ رَحِمَ اللَّهُ جَاراً أَعَانَ جَارَهُ عَلَی بِرِّهِ رَحِمَ اللَّهُ رَفِیقاً أَعَانَ رَفِیقَهُ عَلَی بِرِّهِ رَحِمَ اللَّهُ خَلِیطاً أَعَانَ خَلِیطَهُ عَلَی بِرِّهِ رَحِمَ اللَّهُ رَجُلًا أَعَانَ سُلْطَانَهُ عَلَی بِرِّهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said I was informed by Abdillah b. Ja’far from Harun b. Muslim from Mas’ada b. Ziyad from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father from Ameer al-Mumineen (a.s.) that he said that
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) who said: “Allah showers mercy on the father who helps his son in doing good. He showers mercy on a person who helps his neighbor in doing good. One who helps his friend in doing good Allah showers mercy on the former. Similarly one who helps his companion in doing good, Allah showers mercy on the former too. Also one who helps his master in doing good, Allah showers mercy on that person.”

records.chapter: Reward of spending moderately
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali Majluwayh from Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. al-Hussain from Ja’far b. Basheer from Dawud al-Raqqi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Moderation is best liked by Allah and He does not like extravagance even though it be equal to a few seeds of dates and a little leftover water.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بَشِیرٍ عَنْ دَاوُدَ الرَّقِّیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ الْقَصْدَ أَمْرٌ یُحِبُّهُ اللَّهُ وَ إِنَّ السَّرَفَ أَمْرٌ یُبْغِضُهُ اللَّهُ حَتَّی طَرْحُکَ النَّوَاهَ فَإِنَّهَا تَصْلُحُ لِشَیْ ءٍ وَ حَتَّی صَبُّکَ فَضْلَ شَرَابِکَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali Majluwayh from Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. al-Hussain from Ja’far b. Basheer from Dawud al-Raqqi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Moderation is best liked by Allah and He does not like extravagance even though it be equal to a few seeds of dates and a little leftover water.”

records.chapter: Reward of going on a journey carrying a staff
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  narrates  from  his  predecessors, who narrate from Amirul Momineen (a.s.), who narrates that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “One who goes to a journey and   recites   the   verse:   Wa   lammaa   tawajjaha   tilqaa-a madyana…  till Allaahu  a’laa  maa  naqoolu  wakeel Allah will  protect  him  from  every  wild  animal,  thief,  oppressor and poisonous animals till he returns home. This deed keeps poverty and Satan away. Once Adam (a.s.) became seriously ill  and  began  to  fear.  He  complained  about  it  to  Jibraeel (a.s.) who told him, ‘Take a branch of a bitter almond and keep it on your chest’. He did so and was relieved from the fear  by  Allah. One  who  wants  that the  distance  becomes short for him while traveling should carry a stick of bitter almond.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْجَبَّارِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِیلَ الرَّیَّانِ عَنْ یُونُسَ عَنْ عِدَّهٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبِی عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَنْ أَمِیرِ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ خَرَجَ فِی سَفَرٍ وَ مَعَهُ عَصَا لَوْزٍ مُرٍّ وَ تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآیَهَ- وَ لَمَّا تَوَجَّهَ تِلْقاءَ مَدْیَنَ إِلَی قَوْلِهِ وَ اللَّهُ عَلی ما نَقُولُ وَکِیلٌ آمَنَهُ اللَّهُ مِنْ کُلِّ سَبُعٍ ضَارٍ وَ کُلِّ لِصٍّ عَادٍ وَ کُلِّ ذَاتِ حُمَهٍ حَتَّی یَرْجِعَ إِلَی أَهْلِهِ وَ مَنْزِلِهِ وَ کَانَ مَعَهُ سَبْعَهٌ وَ سَبْعُونَ مِنَ الْمُعَقِّبَاتِ یَسْتَغْفِرُونَ لَهُ حَتَّی یَرْجِعَ وَ یَضَعَهَا.

records.chapter: Reward of starting from home wearing a turban
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan from Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from al-Abbas b. Ma’ruf from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Ali b. Ziyad from a man from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “I guarantee that one who leaves the house wearing a turban will return safe and sound to his family.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارِ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ زِیَادٍ عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: ضَمِنْتُ لِمَنْ یَخْرُجُ مِنْ بَیْتِهِ مُعْتَمّاً أَنْ یَرْجِعَ إِلَیْهِمْ سَالِماً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan from Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from al-Abbas b. Ma’ruf from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Ali b. Ziyad from a man from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “I guarantee that one who leaves the house wearing a turban will return safe and sound to his family.”

records.chapter: Reward of starting from home wearing a turban
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far from Muhammad b. Isa from Abdillah al-Dahqan from Durust from Ibrahim from Abi al-Hassan the first (a.s.) who said: “I guarantee that one who leaves his house wearing a turban will remain safe from theft, drowning and burning (to death).”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الدِّهْقَانِ عَنْ دُرُسْتَ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ أَبِی الْحَسَنِ الْأَوَّلِ ع قَالَ: أَنَا الضَّامِنُ لِمَنْ خَرَجَ مِنْ بَیْتِهِ یُرِیدُ سَفَراً مُعْتَمّاً تَحْتَ حَنَکِهِ أَلَّا یُصِیبَهُ السَّرَقُ وَ الْغَرَقُ وَ الْحَرَقُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far from Muhammad b. Isa from Abdillah al-Dahqan from Durust from Ibrahim from Abi al-Hassan the first (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “I guarantee that one who leaves his house wearing a turban will remain safe from theft, drowning and burning (to death).”

records.chapter: Reward of shedding tears on hearing about Ahle Bayt (a.s.)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan from Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar who said it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Ishaq b. Sa’eed from Bakr b. Muhammad al-Azdi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: 'Do you sit and talk (about our narrations)?' I said, 'May I be sacrificed for you, yes.' He said, 'Indeed, I love those gatherings, so love our matter. Indeed, whoever mentions us and we are mentioned in their presence, and a tear as small as a fly's wing comes out from their eye, Allah forgives their sins, even if they are more than the foam of the sea.'
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ عَنْ بَکْرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الْأَزْدِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: تَجْلِسُونَ وَ تَتَحَدَّثُونَ قَالَ قُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاکَ نَعَمْ قَالَ إِنَّ تِلْکَ الْمَجَالِسَ أُحِبُّهَا فَأَحِبُّوا أَمْرَنَا إِنَّهُ مَنْ ذَکَرَنَا وَ ذُکِرْنَا عِنْدَهُ فَخَرَجَ مِنْ عَیْنِهِ مِثْلُ جَنَاحِ الذُّبَابَهِ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ ذُنُوبَهُ وَ لَوْ کَانَتْ أَکْثَرَ مِنْ زَبَدِ الْبَحْرِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan from Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar who said it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Ishaq b. Sa’eed from Bakr b. Muhammad al-Azdi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'Do you sit and talk (about our narrations)?' I said, 'May I be sacrificed for you, yes.' He said, 'Indeed, I love those gatherings, so love our matter. Indeed, whoever mentions us and we are mentioned in their presence, and a tear as small as a fly's wing comes out from their eye, Allah forgives their sins, even if they are more than the foam of the sea.'

records.chapter: Reward of loving Ahle Bayt (a.s.)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said, “The sins of the  lovers of Ahle Bayt (a.s.) are destroyed in the same way as dry leaves of a tree fall off due to wind.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع إِنَّ حُبَّنَا أَهْلَ الْبَیْتِ لَیَحُطُّ الذُّنُوبَ عَنِ الْعِبَادِ کَمَا یَحُطُّ الرِّیحُ الشَّدِیدَهُ الْوَرَقَ عَنِ الشَّجَرِ.

records.chapter: Reward of fulfilling a desire of a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said, “One who fulfils a desire of a Muslim brother, Allah calls out to the former, ‘Your reward is  with  Me  and  I  won’t  be  satisfied  unless  I  send  you  to Paradise.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع مَا قَضَی مُسْلِمٌ لِمُسْلِمٍ حَاجَهً إِلَّا نَادَاهُ اللَّهُ عَلَیَّ ثَوَابُکَ وَ لَا أَرْضَی لَکَ بِدُونِ الْجَنَّهِ.

records.chapter: Reward of shaking hands with a believing brother
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. And from the previous chain from Bakr b. Ishaq b. Ammar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who is not aware of the status of Allah is not aware of the status of the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) also. And one who is not aware of the status of the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) is not aware of the status of a believer also. If someone sees a believing brother and shakes hands with him, sins are wiped off from their faces like dry leaves fall off a tree due to wind, till they (the believers) separate from one another.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ بَکْرِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا یَقْدِرُ أَحَدٌ قَدْرَهُ فَکَمَا لَا یَقْدِرُ أَحَدٌ قَدْرَهُ کَذَلِکَ لَا یَقْدِرُ أَحَدٌ قَدْرَ نَبِیِّهِ ص فَکَذَلِکَ لَا یَقْدِرُ أَحَدٌ قَدْرَ الْمُؤْمِنِ إِنَّهُ لَیَلْقَی أَخَاهُ فَیُصَافِحُهُ فَیَنْظُرُ اللَّهُ إِلَیْهِمَا وَ الذُّنُوبُ تَحَاتُّ عَنْ وُجُوهِهِمَا حَتَّی یَتَفَرَّقَا کَمَا تَحُطُّ الرِّیحُ الشَّدِیدَهُ الْوَرَقَ عَنِ الشَّجَرِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. And from the previous chain from Bakr b. Ishaq b. Ammar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who is not aware of the status of Allah is not aware of the status of the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) also. And one who is not aware of the status of the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) is not aware of the status of a believer also. If someone sees a believing brother and shakes hands with him, sins are wiped off from their faces like dry leaves fall off a tree due to wind, till they (the believers) separate from one another.”

records.chapter: Reward of praising and glorifying Allah after receiving bounties
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said, “O Abu Ishaq! When Allah grants a bounty to His servant and he recognizes it with his heart and he praises and glorifies Allah openly, Allah will grant  him  a  better  bounty  as  soon  as  this  bounty  gets finished.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع مَنْ أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَیْهِ بِنِعْمَهٍ فَعَرَفَهَا بِقَلْبِهِ وَ جَهَرَ بِحَمْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَیْهَا فَفَرَغَ مِنْهَا حَتَّی یُؤْمَرَ لَهُ بِالْمَزِیدِ.

records.chapter: Reward of living a life of forty to ninety years
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It is narrated that Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said, “When a person reaches the age of forty, Allah keeps him safe from three diseases like insanity, leprosy and white spots. When he  reaches  the  age  of  fifty,  Allah  curtails  his  accounting. When he reaches the age of sixty, Allah makes him return to Himself. When he reaches the age of seventy, the people of heavens  start  loving  him.  When  he  reaches  the  age  of eighty, Allah orders  that  his  good deeds  be  written down and sins not be written. When he reaches the age of ninety, Allah, the Mighty and Sublime forgives his past and present sins and writes him as a prisoner of Allah on His earth.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الْعَطَّارُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارِ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِی نَجْرَانَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْمُغِیرَهِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ یَقُولُ إِذَا بَلَغَ الْمَرْءُ أَرْبَعِینَ سَنَهً آمَنَهُ اللَّهُ مِنَ الْأَدْوَاءِ الثَّلَاثَهِ الْجُنُونِ وَ الْجُذَامِ وَ الْبَرَصِ فَإِذَا بَلَغَ الْخَمْسِینَ خَفَّفَ اللَّهُ حِسَابَهُ فَإِذَا بَلَغَ السِّتِّینَ رَزَقَهُ اللَّهُ الْإِنَابَهَ إِلَیْهِ فَإِذَا بَلَغَ السَّبْعِینَ أَحَبَّهُ أَهْلُ السَّمَاءِ فَإِذَا بَلَغَ الثَّمَانِینَ أَمَرَ اللَّهُ بِإِثْبَاتِ حَسَنَاتِهِ وَ إِلْقَاءِ سَیِّئَاتِهِ فَإِذَا بَلَغَ التِّسْعِینَ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ مَا تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ ذَنْبِهِ وَ مَا تَأَخَّرَ وَ کُتِبَ أَسِیرَ اللَّهِ فِی أَرْضِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  It is narrated that Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “When a person reaches the age of forty, Allah keeps him safe from three diseases like insanity, leprosy and white spots. When he  reaches  the  age  of  fifty,  Allah  curtails  his  accounting. When he reaches the age of sixty, Allah makes him return to Himself. When he reaches the age of seventy, the people of heavens  start  loving  him.  When  he  reaches  the  age  of eighty, Allah orders  that  his  good deeds  be  written down and sins not be written. When he reaches the age of ninety, Allah, the Mighty and Sublime forgives his past and present sins and writes him as a prisoner of Allah on His earth.”

records.chapter: Reward of living a life of forty to ninety years
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Salamah b. al-Khattab from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Abdulrahman from Ismail b. Abd al-Khaliq from Muhammad b. Talha from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Surely Allah honors a person aged seventy and grants modesty to a person aged seventy.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ سَلَمَهَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِیلَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْخَالِقِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ طَلْحَهَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَیُکْرِمُ أَبْنَاءَ التِّسْعِینَ وَ یَسْتَحِی مِنْ أَبْنَاءِ الثَّمَانِینَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Salamah b. al-Khattab from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Abdulrahman from Ismail b. Abd al-Khaliq from Muhammad b. Talha from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Surely Allah honors a person aged seventy and grants modesty to a person aged seventy.”

records.chapter: Reward of living a life of forty to ninety years
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 3. And from the previous chain from Salamah from Ali b. al-Hassan from Ahmad b. Muhammad Mu’addab from ’Asim b. Hameed from Khalid al-Qalanisi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Allah feels ashamed to punish a person aged eighty. An elderly person will be brought on the Day of Judgment and his scroll of deeds will be handed over to him. People will see nothing in it except unpleasant things. He will say, ‘O Lord! Will you throw me also into Hell?’ Allah will reply, ‘O elderly! You used to offer Salaat in the world for. I feel ashamed to throw you in Hell’, and he will be ordered to be taken to Paradise.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ سَلَمَهَ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الْمُؤَدِّبِ عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ حُمَیْدٍ عَنْ خَالِدٍ الْقَلَانِسِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ یَسْتَحِی مِنْ أَبْنَاءِ الثَّمَانِینَ أَنْ یُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَ قَالَ ع یُؤْتَی بِشَیْخٍ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ فَیُدْفَعُ إِلَیْهِ کِتَابُهُ ظَاهِرُهُ فِیمَا یَلِی النَّاسَ لَا یَرَی إِلَّا مَسَاوِیَ فَیَطُولُ ذَلِکَ عَلَیْهِ فَیَقُولُ یَا رَبِّ أَ تُعِیدُنِی إِلَی النَّارِ فَیَقُولُ الْجَبَّارُ جَلَّ جَلَالُهُ یَا شَیْخُ إِنِّی أَسْتَحِی أَنْ أُعَذِّبَکَ وَ قَدْ کُنْتَ تُصَلِّی لِی فِی دَارِ الدُّنْیَا اذْهَبُوا بِعَبْدِی إِلَی الْجَنَّهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3. And from the previous chain from Salamah from Ali b. al-Hassan from Ahmad b. Muhammad Mu’addab from ’Asim b. Hameed from Khalid al-Qalanisi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Allah feels ashamed to punish a person aged eighty. An elderly person will be brought on the Day of Judgment and his scroll of deeds will be handed over to him. People will see nothing in it except unpleasant things. He will say, ‘O Lord! Will you throw me also into Hell?’ Allah will reply, ‘O elderly! You used to offer Salaat in the world for. I feel ashamed to throw you in Hell’, and he will be ordered to be taken to Paradise.”

records.chapter: Reward of respecting an elderly person after knowing his excellence
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “One who knows the excellence of an elderly person and honors him, Allah, the Mighty and Sublime will grant him salvation from fear on the Day of Judgment.” And he said, “Respecting an elderly believer is a way of showing respect to Allah, the Mighty and Sublime.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَلَمَهُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ حَسَّانَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ أُمَیَّهَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ یَرْفَعُهُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ عَرَفَ فَضْلَ شَیْخٍ کَبِیرٍ فَوَقَّرَهُ لِسِنِّهِ آمَنَهُ اللَّهُ مِنْ فَزَعِ یَوْمِ الْقِیَامَهِ وَ قَالَ مِنْ تَعْظِیمِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِجْلَالُ ذِی الشَّیْبَهِ الْمُؤْمِنِ.

records.chapter: Reward of Jihad in the way of Allah along with a just Imam
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said that Jibraeel (a.s.) told him, “O  Muhammad  (s.a.w.s.)!  One  who  from  your  Ummah participates in a battle in the way of Allah, each and every drop of rain falling  from the  sky will bear  witness  on the Day of Judgment to the trouble he had to take.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِیِّ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ وَهْبِ بْنِ وَهْبٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص إِنَّ جَبْرَئِیلَ ع أَخْبَرَنِی بِأَمْرٍ فَقَرَّتْ بِهِ عَیْنِی وَ فَرِحَ بِهِ قَلْبِی قَالَ یَا مُحَمَّدُ مَنْ غَزَا غَزْوَهً فِی سَبِیلِ اللَّهِ مِنْ أُمَّتِکَ فَمَا أَصَابَتْهُ قَطْرَهٌ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ أَوْ صُدَاعٌ إِلَّا کَانَ لَهُ شَهَادَهٌ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said that
records.thaqalaynMatn: Jibraeel (a.s.) told him, “O  Muhammad  (s.a.w.s.)!  One  who  from  your  Ummah participates in a battle in the way of Allah, each and every drop of rain falling  from the  sky will bear  witness  on the Day of Judgment to the trouble he had to take.”
